summary: Replace 9 zero-variance scorers with gradient scoring across 6 scenario files
context: Full 440-run benchmark revealed 9 scoring dimensions always returning fixed values (0 or 100), suppressing the signal needed to compare coordination conditions. Root cause: concatenated file paths + diffs with overly broad regex patterns (e.g. /Error/i matches any TypeScript code).
rationale: Each scorer rewritten with: (1) separated file-path vs diff-content checks, (2) added-lines-only matching to avoid false positives from context, (3) multiple graduated levels (0/25/50/75/100) instead of binary, (4) specific patterns requiring substantive implementation (not just keyword existence). Scale-stress-test orientationOverhead changed from linear to bell-curve sweet-spot model.

alternatives.reason_rejected: The dimensions measure real aspects of coordination quality. The scoring logic was broken, not the concepts. Gradient replacements preserve measurement intent while producing usable variance.
